title: Simulate Radioactive Decay
description: Build a Monte Carlo simulation of radioactive decay using random numbers to model the probabilistic nature of nuclear decay. Derive the exponential decay law from first principles of probability.
whyItMatters: Radioactive decay is inherently probabilistic — no one can predict when a specific atom will decay. This project teaches Monte Carlo methods, probability, exponential functions, and the statistical nature of quantum processes.

steps.title: Model single-atom decay
steps.description: Simulate a single atom: at each time step, generate a random number. If it is below the decay probability p, the atom decays. Run 1000 trials and plot the distribution of decay times.
steps.tips: The decay probability per time step p is related to the half-life by p = 1 - 2^(-dt/t_half).

steps.title: Simulate a sample of N atoms
steps.description: Start with N = 10,000 atoms. At each time step, each undecayed atom has probability p of decaying. Track the number of remaining atoms vs time.
steps.tips: Use NumPy vectorisation for speed — do not loop over individual atoms.

steps.title: Fit the exponential
steps.description: Plot N(t) vs t and fit an exponential N(t) = N_0 * exp(-lambda*t). Extract lambda and calculate the half-life. Compare to the input value.
steps.tips: Use scipy.optimize.curve_fit for the exponential fit.

steps.title: Explore statistical fluctuations
steps.description: Repeat the simulation for N = 100, 1000, and 10,000 atoms. Observe how the curve becomes smoother as N increases — the law of large numbers in action.
steps.tips: Plot all three on the same axes to see the effect of sample size.

steps.title: Add a decay chain
steps.description: Simulate A decaying into B, which then decays into stable C. Track all three populations over time and compare to the Bateman equations.
steps.tips: This models real decay chains like uranium-238 to lead-206.

deliverables: Comparison of statistical fluctuations for different sample sizes
Decay chain simulation with three species

rubric.description: Decay probability correctly implemented, exponential decay reproduced
Proper fitting, extracted half-life matches input within uncertainty
Multi-species decay correctly implemented and compared to theory
